items.title: A Sydney mother died after drinking 67 cups of water under 24-hour watch

children.content: When Tammy Shipley felt herself sliding into psychosis, she made a heartbreaking decision: She walked into a police station and asked to be arrested, believing prison would keep her safe.

gallery.altText: A mother holds a boy in her arms. They both smile. The boy wears an orange and red jumper.

items.title: Anthony Albanese has shown he understands the Westminster system

children.content: This week the Coalition's weakness in Question Time reminded onlookers of Albanese's command of the Westminster system, while Labor insiders expressed their anxieties about One Nation's polling surge.

image.altText: Albanese looking down in a suit while touching his ear.

items.title: Buyer's agency Dashdot collapse leaves hundreds of clients out of pocket

children.content: Liquidators say at least 700 Dashdot clients could be caught up in the buyer's agency collapse, with some who paid large up-front fees fearing they may not get their money back.

image.altText: Man wearing blue jumper and shorts and a grey cap stands outside a shed, looking disappointed.

items.title: Fastest-rising women's cancer in Australia still has a low profile

children.content: The incidence of the cancer has doubled over the past 25 years. But it's also a disease without a public profile, which means many women aren't aware of the warning signs or the risk factors.

image.altText: A composite image of a baby in a trolley next to a portrait image of a woman smiling.

items.title: Fresh concerns emerge over impact of Labor's tax changes on medical tech

image.altText: A person in a lab coat doing a test with a small tool.

items.title: The fake police scam stealing millions from Australia's Chinese community

children.content: Justin thought he was being investigated by the Chinese police, but was pulled into a vicious and frightening scam targeting the Chinese diaspora all over the world.

gallery.altText: A Chinese man with round glasses looks at a computer

items.title: A US ally is getting nuclear-powered submarines with no AUKUS. Here's how

image.altText: Three men in white uniforms and caps salute at the entrance on top of a large grey submarine, with a South Korean flag behind.

items.title: 'Worried about my seat': NSW premier thinks One Nation is a 'threat'

children.content: NSW Premier Chris Minns is enjoying growing support in his electorate, but is still concerned about Pauline Hanson's presence in next year's state election.

items.title: Could a 'mansion tax' help the housing crisis?

children.content: Amid some of the most drastic proposed changes to Australia's tax system in decades and years of skyrocketing home values, one leading expert says it is an idea "worth considering".

items.title: Acid attack victim's lawyer describes trial of perpetrators as a 'sham'

children.content: Four military officers are facing trial over an acid attack on an Indonesian human rights campaigner that left him seriously disfigured. But some lawyers say the legal process is a "sham".

items.title: Andrew made money subletting cottages on his almost rent-free estate, watchdog finds

children.content: Former prince Andrew pocketed income from cottages on the Windsor estate while living at Royal Lodge under a lease that required virtually no rent, a watchdog report has found.

items.title: The 20-minute coffee van that keeps a small community together

items.title: Need a mood boost? Here's why gardening might be worth a try

children.content: While gardening alone is not a treatment for mental illness, a growing body of evidence links it with higher levels of mental wellbeing and life satisfaction.
